response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine geospatial domain familiarity through GISCollective and Green Map work, and solid distributed systems exposure from high-throughput micro-services at Sociomantic. However, his C++ experience is limited to a brief six-month role at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project; there is no Rust experience at all. His career has moved firmly into TypeScript/Node.js web stacks, diverging substantially from the C++-primary, Linux systems-level requirements of this role. Critically, he has no demonstrated experience with navigation guidance, routing algorithms, or routing tile pipelines, which are the core deliverables of the position.
strengths: Geospatial domain background through GISCollective (open-source event-driven Geo-Data platform) and Green Map GIS application, including a FOSS4G conference talk
Distributed systems and high-throughput pipeline experience at Sociomantic, building low-latency async I/O services handling millions of requests per second
Consistent software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and quality-assurance practices applied across multiple roles
concerns: C++ experience is dated (2014–2015) and narrow in scope; no Rust experience anywhere in the resume
No navigation, routing, or map tile pipeline work — the central technical domain of the role
Career trajectory has moved away from systems programming toward web and TypeScript stacks, misaligning with the C++-primary, Linux server application focus required
